{VRotate{x allows you to switch from one target to another in the middle of
battle.

Training in {Vsecond attack{x allows the character a chance at additional
strikes in combat -- although a 100% {Vsecond attack{x does NOT guarantee
2 attacks every round.

{VShield block{x is a rather fancy name for the art of parrying with a shield.
Characters with no shield block skill will not be able to defend themselves
well with a shield.  Beware, flails ignore shield blocking attempts, and
whips have an easier time getting around them.  Axes may split shields in two.

Syntax: {Vsing{x <{Yvictim{x>

Singing is a bard-type skill that allows one to charm their adoring
listeners.  Unlike the {Ccharm{x spell, failure simply means that your
song wasn't good enough - the victim won't be provoked to attack you.

Syntax: {Vsteal coins{x <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Vsteal{x <{Yobject{x> <{Ycharacter{x>

{VSteal{x attempts to steal coins, or an object, from a character.  There
are penalties for using {Vsteal{x on other players.  In order to {Vsteal{x
successfully, you must practice the appropriate skill.

Players are allowed to steal from other players if both have {RPK{x turned
on and both players are within {Y8{x levels of each other.  It is possible
for someone to steal from you while fighting, sleeping, or just standing
around.

If you are caught stealing, you will receive a {PTHIEF{x flag.  Beware!

Most players and most mobiles rapidly lose hit points when {Bunderwater{x
{Yunless{x they are skilled at {Vswimming{x.  A player with {V100% swimming skill{x
can safely remain {Bunderwater{x indefinitely.

Training in {Vthird attack{x allows the character a chance at an additional
strike in a combat, and increases the chance of a second attack as well.
Perfect {Vthird attack{x does NOT assure three attacks per round.

Syntax: {Vtrip{x <{Ytarget{x>

{VTrip{x is a somewhat dastardly attack, and involves using any one of a
number of methods to bring your opponent down to the ground.  Tripping
large monsters is generally not a good idea, and agile ones will find
the attack easy to avoid.

Syntax: {Vlay{x <{Ytarget{x>
Syntax: {Vlay{x {Ycount{x

Lay is a Paladin skill which allows you to use your Power of Faith
to heal other people. A Paladin may perform Lay on Hands a few
times per day on other players. This skill does not allow a Paladin
to heal him or herself.
The number of times a Paladin may perform Lay on Hands and its
power is determined by a character's level.

Magical items require training to use properly.  If your character lacks the
necessary skill to use an item, he will fail, possibly destroying it.  The
item skills are as follows:

{Vscrolls{x   The reading of magical scrolls and books (see '{Yhelp recite{x')
{Vstaves{x    The use of staves and similar devices (see '{Yhelp brandish{x')
{Vwands{x     The use of wands and similar items (see '{Yhelp zap{x')

keywords: WEAPONSMASTER AXE DAGGER EXOTIC FLAIL MACE POLEARM SPEAR SWORD WHIP
text:
Each {Rweapon skill{x applies to a specific group of armaments, and determines how
well a character fights with a particular weapon.  The weaponsmaster group
provides talent in all weapons (save exotics), from chair legs to halberds.
If the character has the parry skill as well, he can parry incoming attacks.
The chance of parrying is best if the character in question is skilled at
both his weapon and his opponent's.

The weapon skills consist of the following:

{Rweaponsmaster{x  skill group of all weapons listed below (save exotic weaponry)
{Raxe{x            the use of axes, ranging from hand to great (but not halberds)
{Rdagger{x         the use of knives and daggers, and other stabbing weapons
{Rflail{x          skill in ball-and-chain type weapons
{Rmace{x           this skill includes clubs and hammers as well as maces
{Rpolearm{x        the use of pole weapons (except spears), including halberds
{Rspear{x          this skill covers both spears and staves, but not polearms
{Rsword{x          the warrior's standby, from rapier to claymore
{Rwhip{x           the use of whips, chains, and bullwhips
{Rexotic{x         the use of strange magical weapons

The {Rexotic{x skill cannot be purchased, and is dependent solely upon level.

Syntax: {Cconvert{x <{Yskill_points{x>
The convert command allows you to change skill points into
practice sessions. Each new practice session costs 10 skill points.

Syntax: {Cpreserve{x <{Yitem{x>

Sometimes, to save food, or sometimes to make sure that arm or heart
of your foe stays good for a long time, you need to {Ypreserve{x it.
The preserve skill allows you to keep items (such as corpse pieces)
from rotting away.

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yspell{x' <{Ytarget{x>

Before you can cast a spell, you have to learn it by practicing it.  The
more you practice, the higher chance you have of success when casting.
Casting spells costs mana.

The <{Ytarget{x> is optional.  Many spells which need targets will use an
appropriate default target, especially during combat.

If the spell name is more than one word, then you must quote the spell name.
Example: {Ccast{x '{Ycure critical{x' frag.  Quoting is optional for single word
spells.  You can abbreviate the spell name.

When you cast an offensive spell, the victim usually gets a saving throw.
The effect of the spell is reduced or eliminated if the victim makes the
saving throw successfully.

{YSEE ALSO:{x the help files for individual spells.

The {Rattack spells{x are essentially powerful curses, fueled by divine energy
rather than magical power (like the combat spells are). They are less powerful
than the combat spells, but can still deal out a stinging blow.  
The {Rattack spell group{x consists of the following spells:

{Cdemonfire{x     a powerful, but very evil, spell
{Cdispel evil{x   torments evil foes
{Cdispel good{x   calls down unholy power on good creatures
{Cearthquake{x    brings the power of earth to bear against your foes
{Cflamestrike{x   sends a column of flame from the heavens
{Cray of truth{x  sends forth a blinding ray of holy energy
{Csunray{x        calls down a divine searing beam of light.
{Cheat metal{x    makes metal armor very hot

Syntax: {Ccast{x {Ydemonfire{x <{Ytarget{x>

{CDemonfire{x is a spell of blackest evil, and as such can only be used by
those who follow the paths of darkness.  It conjures forth demonic spirits
to inflict terrible wounds on the enemies of the caster.

GROUP: {RAttack{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydispel evil{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell invokes the wrath of Mota on an evil victim. It can be very
dangerous for casters who are evil.

GROUP: {PAttack{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydispel good{x' <{Yvictim{x>

{CDispel good{x brings forth evil energies that inflict horrific torment on
the pure of heart.  Good-aligned characters use this dark magic at their
peril.

GROUP: {RAttack{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yearthquake{x'

This spell inflicts damage on every enemy character in the room that
has their feet on the ground.  Beware that other characters who are
not yet fighting may attack you as a result!

GROUP: {RAttack{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yflamestrike{x' <{Yvictim{x>

{CFlamestrike{x invokes a bolt of fire to inflict damage on the victim.

GROUP:	{RAttack{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yheat metal{x' <{Ytarget{x>

{CHeat metal{x is a powerful clerical attack spell, with effects that vary
according to the armor of the victim.  It heats up the metal equipment
(assumed to be all weapons and armor at this point in time) on the target,
causing him or her to drop them if possible, taking serious burns in the
process (possibly fatal if the equipment is too heavy to remove easily).
This spell does no damage to creatures who are immune to fire.

GROUP: {RAlteration{x
GROUP: {RAttack{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yray of truth{x' <{Ytarget{x>

{RRay of truth{x opens a portal to the planes of positive energy, bringing forth
a beam of light of sufficient purity to harm or or annihilate the servants
of evil.  It cannot harm the pure of heart, and will turn and strike
casters who are tainted by evil.

GROUP: {RAttack{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ysunray{x' <{Ytarget{x>

{CSunray{x is the most powerful of a clerics offensive arsenal, bringing down
a divine light that sears the cleric's enemies both good and evil in nature.
Used wisely, this will help clerics and paladins across Thera spread justice
and order.

GROUP: {RAttack{x

This insidious spell group is used to invade the minds of others.  It
consists of various charming magics, well suited for turning your foes
to your cause.  The {Rbeguiling spell group{x consist of the following:

{Ccalm{x          if successful, stops all fighting in the room
{Ccharm person{x  turns an enemy into a trusted friend
{Csleep{x         puts a foe into enchanted slumber
{Cpower word{x    with one word, die, your victim will obey

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycharm person{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell, if successful, causes the victim to follow and to take
orders from you.  Use {WORDER{x to order your charmed followers.  You
are responsible for the actions of your followers.

GROUP: {RBeguiling{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ysleep{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell causes the victim to fall into a magically induced slumber.
They cannot be woken from this slumber while the spell is in affect
unless they take damage.

GROUP: {RBeguiling{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ypower word{x' <{Yvictim{x>

With a single word, the caster can cause a victim to die instantly.  Only
lower level creatures are suspectible to death from {Cpower word{x.

GROUP: {RBeguiling{x

{RBenedictions{x are priestly spells, used to bestow divine favor upon your
allies.  They are sometimes restricted to targets of like alignment.    
The {Rbenedictions spell group{x consists of the following:

{Cbless{x         bestows divine favor upon the target
{Ccalm{x          if successful, stops all fighting in the room
{Cfrenzy{x        puts the recipient into beserker rage
{Choly word{x     aids your allies while calling divine wrath upon your foes
{Cremove curse{x  removes malevolent magic from players and items
{Cremove invis{x  makes invisible items visible
{Cremove align{x  removes the alignment from objects

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ybless{x <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ybless{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell improves the to-hit roll and saving throw versus spell of the
target character by 1 for every 8 levels of the caster. It may also be
cast on an object to temporarily bless it (blessed weapons, for example,
are more effective against demonic beings).  Bless may be cast only on
characters of good alignment and never on fighting characters.

GROUP: {RBenedictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycalm{x'

One of the most useful and often overlooked abilities of the master cleric
is the {Ccalm{x spell, which can put an end to all violence in a room.  Calmed
creatures will not attack of their own volition, and are at a disadvantage
in combat as long as the spell soothes their minds.  The more violent
activity there is in a room, the harder the spell, and it is all or nothing
-- either all combat in the room is ended or none of it is.

GROUP: {RBenedictions{x
GROUP: {RBeguiling{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yfrenzy{x' <{Ytarget{x>

The {Cfrenzy{x spell fills the target with righteous fury, greatly increasing
his or her attack skill and damaging capacity.  Unfortunately, this divine
wrath is coupled with a tendency to ignore threats to personal safety,
making the character easier to hit.  {CFrenzy{x provides immunity to the calm
spell, and may only be used on those of the caster's alignment.

GROUP: {RBenedictions{x
{YSEE ALSO:{x calm, berserk

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yholy word{x'

{CHoly word{x is the invocation of the full power of a cleric's god, with
disastrous effects upon the enemies of the priest coupled with powerful
blessings on the priest's allies.  All creatures of like alignment in the
room are blessed and filled with righteous divine wrath, while those of
opposite morals (or both good and evil in the case of neutral priests)
are struck down by holy (or unholy might) and cursed.  The cleric suffers
greatly from the strain of this spell, being left unable to move and
drained of vitality.  Experience loss is no longer associated with the spell.

GROUP: {RBenedictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yremove curse{x' <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yremove curse{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell removes a {Ccurse{x from a character, and might possibly uncurse
a cursed object.  It may also be targeted on an object in the caster's
inventory, in which case its chance of success is significantly higher.

GROUP: {RBenedictions{x

The {Rweaponcraft spell group{x is the art of magically altering the abilities 
of weapons.  The {Rbladecraft spell group{x consist of the following:

{Cflame blade{x   adds the power of fire to your weapons.
{Cfrost blade{x   adds the power of frost to your weapons.
{Cblood blade{x   adds the power of energy drain to your weapons.
{Cshock blade{x   adds the power of lightning to your weapons.

keywords: FLAME BLADE' 'FROST BLADE' 'BLOOD BLADE' 'SHOCK BLADE' BLADE
text:
Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yflame blade{x' <{Yweapon{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yfrost blade{x' <{Yweapon{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yblood blade{x' <{Yweapon{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yshock blade{x' <{Yweapon{x>

These spells add weapon flags to your favorite weapons for a limited
period of time.  Only one bladecraft spell may be cast on a weapon at
any time.

GROUP: {RBladecraft{x

These sorcerous spells are, in the words of one famous mage, just 'more
ways to toss energy around'.  They are the most powerful of the damaging
spells, and considered an essential part of most wizards' collections. 
In order of power, the {Rcombat spell group{x contains the following magics:

{Cmagic missile{x    send a weak energy bolt into a foe
{Cchill touch{x      weakens your enemy with a frigid grasp
{Cburning hands{x    sends a blast of fire into your foe
{Cshocking grasp{x   sends a powerful jolt into a foe
{Clightning bolt{x   sends forth a single bolt of lightning into an enemy
{Ccolour spray{x     blasts your opponent with a blinding rainbow spray
{Cfireball{x         a powerful spell, great for burning your enemy to ashes
{Cacid blast{x       sends forth a sphere of acid to eradicate your foes
{Cchain lightning{x  sends lightning bolts arcing through foes
{Cfirestorm{x        sends out arcing fire to your foes
{Cwrath{x            a devastating manifestation of raw magical power

Syntax: {Ccast {x'{Ymagic missile{x' <{Ytarget{x>

{CMagic missile{x is first spell available to any student of combat magic,
being the simplest and most direct conversion of magical power into
an offensive attack.  The caster conjures a small bolt of force which
flies into an opponent.  At higher levels, a caster develops greater
willpower, and can conjure up multiple {Cmagic missiles{x with a single
casting.  As with all combat spells, specifiying a target is optional
when in combat; if you are fighting, casting without a target will direct
the spell at your primary opponent.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax: {Ccast {x'{Ychill touch{x' <{Ytarget{x>

To cast {Cchill touch{x, a caster surrounds a finger with a cloud of
freezing mist, and jabs the finger at an opponent.  The magically
conjured vapor clings to the target and settles in, destroying a small
section of tissue.  Occasionally, the touch will strike a vital area
and cause the victim to lose strength.  As with all combat spells,
specifiying a target is optional when in combat; if you are fighting,
casting without a target will direct the spell at your primary opponent.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax: {Ccast {x'{Yburning hands{x' <{Ytarget{x>

To cast {Cburning hands{x, a caster engulfs both hands with flames and
drives them into an opponent.  As with all combat spells, specifiying
a target is optional when in combat; if you are fighting, casting
without a target will direct the spell at your primary opponent.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax: {Ccast {x'{Yshocking grasp{x' <{Ytarget{x>

Similar to {Cchill touch{x and {Cburning hands{x, {Cshocking grasp{x involves the
caster enveloping their hands with magic power.  In this case, charges
of lightning are conjured and delivered by grasping the opponent.  As
with all combat spells, specifiying a target is optional when in combat;
if you are fighting, casting without a target will direct the spell at
your primary opponent.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax: {Ccast {x'{Ylightning bolt{x' <{Ytarget{x>

{CLightning bolt{x is one of the classic spells in a magic-users arsenal
of offensive weapons.  Ancient studies of weather elementals revealed
that magic users could create extreme charge differentials between
themselves and a target.  Magically encouraging the release of this
stored energy causes what is essentially an oversized {Wstatic shock{x,
zapping the target but somehow not causing harm to the caster.  As
with all combat spells, specifiying a target is optional when in
combat; if you are fighting, casting without a target will direct
the spell at your primary opponent.

GROUP: {RCombat{x
GROUP: {RWeather{x

Syntax: {Ccast {x'{Ycolour spray{x' <{Ytarget{x>

Just like Jubilee's mutant power in X-Men!

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yfireball{x' <{Ytarget{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yfireball{x' {Groom{x                      ({YEvolution 2{x)

One of the most basic and universal combat spells, {Cfireball{x involves
the conjuration of a sphere of fire and hurling it at an enemy.  It's
also one of the more versatile combat spells.  With the encouragement
of {WL{Ci{Br{Ci{Te{Wl{x, the archmage {NS{By{Tv{Ce{Tr{Bi{Nl{x has developed advanced modifications
for {Cfireball{x.

{GEvolved:{x
When evolved, the {Cfireball{x spell becomes progressively more powerful
and larger, allowing the caster to engulf an entire room with their
{Hf{Gir{Web{Gal{Hl{x.  At higher evolution, a {Bf{Cir{Web{Cal{Bl{x is infused with explosive
power, capable of knocking down enemies.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax: {Ccast {x'{Yacid blast{x' <{Ytarget{x>

With this spell, the caster encases a globe of corrosive acid within
a sphere of force and hurls it at an enemy.  The sphere explodes upon
impact, causing grievous injury to the victim.  As with all combat
spells, specifiying a target is optional when in combat; if you are
fighting, casting without a target will direct the spell at your
primary opponent.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax : {Ccast{x '{Ychain lightning{x' <{Ytarget{x>

{CChain lightning{x is a deadly spell, producing a powerful bolt of
lightning that arcs from target to target in the room, until its force
is fully expended.  Killable players will also be hit by this spell
unless they are grouped with the caster, while the caster himself will
not be struck unless no other viable target remains.  {CChain lightning{x
is most effective when used on groups of creatures.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax:  {Ccast {x'{Yfirestorm{x' <{Ytarget{x>

{CFirestorm{x is very similar to {cchain lightning{x, as it is a fire-based
variant on that spell.  A huge flame arcs out of the caster's hand in
response to a casting of {Cfirestorm{x, and is considered even more deadly
than the {Cchain lightning{x spell.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

Syntax:  {Ccast {x'{Ywrath{x' <{Ytarget{x>

The ultimate manifestation of a caster's ability to translate magical
power into devastation, {Cwrath{x bypasses the crutches of elemental fury and
delivers large amounts of explosive force to an opponent.  To be sure,
this requires great willpower and only magic users with a great deal of
experience can attempt to cast {Cwrath{x.  As with all combat spells,
specifiying a target is optional when in combat; if you are fighting,
casting without a target will direct the spell at your primary opponent.

GROUP: {RCombat{x

The {Rcreation spell group{x is used to create objects, of temporary or permanent
duration.  Skilled creators can travel without food or drink, and use their
powers to create nourishment as required.
The {Rcreation spell group{x contains the following:

{Ccontinual light{x   creates an eternal light source
{Ccreate food{x       produces a nourishing mushroom
{Ccreate rose{x       creates a beautiful red rose
{Ccreate spring{x     calls forth a small but pure spring from the ground
{Ccreate water{x      fills any available container with water
{Cfloating disc{x     creates a floating disc of force to hold items
{Ccreate vial{x       creates a vial for brewing potions
{Ccreate parchment{x  creates a parchment for scribing scrolls
{Cencampment{x        creates a small campsite for one person
{Ccreate sign{x       creates a magical sign for all to read

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycontinual light{x'
        {Ccast{x '{Ycontinual light{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell creates a ball of light which you can hold as a light source.
The ball of light will last indefinitely, and may also be used on an object
to give it an enchanted glow.

GROUP: {RCreation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycreate vial{x'
        {Ccast{x '{Ycreate parchment{x'

These spells create items for brewing and scribing.

GROUP: {RCreation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yencampment{x'

This spell creates a magical campsite for the traveling player.

GROUP: {RCreation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycreate sign{x' <{Ymessage{x>

This spell creates a magical sign which others can see and read.

GROUP: {RCreation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycreate food{x' <{PFood name{x>

This spell creates an item of food, which you or anyone else can eat.
Like create rose, this spell can be used without an arguement, and
will create a random food type of the Legacy Imm's favourite foods,
or you can add your own flavor text (get the pun?), but be warned, as
with other spells with message arguments, please use discretion so as
to not to cause offense. {R:){x

GROUP: {RCreation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycreate spring{x'
        {Ccast{x '{Ycreate water{x' <{Ydrink-container{x>

{CCreate spring{x brings forth a magical spring from the ground which has the
same properties as a fountain (although it will eventually run dry).

{CCreate water{x replenishes a drinking container with water.
GROUP: {RCreation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycreate rose{x' <{Ycolor{x>

A romantic spell that creates a fragrant rose, with utterly no game
use whatsoever.  The default color is {Rred{x.

GROUP: {RCreation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yfloating disc{x'

This useful spell creates a floating field of force which follows the
caster around, allowing him or her to pile treasure high with no fear
of weight penalties.  It lasts no more than twice the casters level in
hours, and usually less.  It can hold 10 pounds per level of the caster,
with a maximum of five pounds per item.  The spell requires an open float
location on the character, and the only way to remove the disc is to die
or allow it to run out of energy.

GROUP: {RCreation{x

The {Rcurative spells{x are used to heal various unpleasant conditions that can
befall an adventurer.   For healing of damage, see the healing spell group.
The {Rcurative spell group{x contains the following:

{Ccure blindness{x  restored sight to the blind
{Ccure disease{x    heals the plague
{Ccure poison{x     removes the harmful effects of poison

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycure blindness{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell cures blindness in one so unfortunate.

GROUP: {RCurative{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycure poison{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell cures a character's poison, or your own poison if you don't
specify a target.

GROUP: {RCurative{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycure disease{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell cures a character's plague, or your own plague if you don't
specify a target.

GROUP: {RCurative{x

The {Rdetection spells{x have many uses, all related to gathering information. They
can be used to see hidden objects, give information about treasure, or even
scry out the true nature of your foe. 
The {Rdetection spell group{x contains the following:

{Cdetect evil{x     reveals the aura of evil monsters
{Cdetect good{x     similar to above, only for good monsters
{Cdetect hidden{x   shows the whereabouts of hidden foes
{Cdetect invis{x    allows the caster to see the unseeable
{Cdetect magic{x    reveals magical auras to the caster
{Cdetect poison{x   determines if food is safe to eat
{Cfarsight{x        allows to caster to see farther about him
{Cidentify{x        gives information about the nature of an object
{Cknow alignment{x  determines the moral character of a monster or person
{Clocate object{x   finds a specific item
{Clight of truth{x  enhances the detection capabilities of a light
{Clocate life{x     searches out through the lands for a specific lifeform
{Cvision{x          lets you see the contents of a room from afar

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydetect evil{x'

This spell enables the caster to detect evil characters, who are revealed
by a characteristic red aura.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydetect hidden{x'
        {Ccast{x '{Ydetect invis{x'
        
{CDetect hidden{x enables the caster to detect hidden creatures.

{CDetect invis{x will enable the caster to detect invisible objects,
monsters, and players.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydetect magic{x'

This spell enables the caster to detect magical objects.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydetect poison{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell detects the presence of poison in food or drink.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydetect good{x'

This spell enables the caster to detect good characters, who are revealed
by a characteristic golden aura.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x {Yfarsight{x
        {Ccast{x {Yfarsight{x <{Ydirection{x>

The {Cfarsight{x spell expands the caster's consciousness, allowing him or
her to see far away beings like they were in the same room.  The spell may
be used for a general scan that reaches a short distance in all directions,
or with a directional component to see creatures much farther away.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yidentify{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell reveals valuable information about the object.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yknow alignment{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell reveals the alignment of the target character.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ylight of truth{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell puts random detection spells on a light source for a limited time.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ylocate object{x' <{Yname{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ylocate life{x' <{Yvictim{x>

{CLocate object{x reveals the location of all objects with the given name.

{CLocate life{x reveals the location of all characters with the given name.
Both spells have some limitations to their usefulness, and neither can
detect objects or players that are higher level than the caster.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yvision{x' <{Ytarget{x>

This vision spell creates a mystical sphere from which you can look inside
to see the location of your target.  The target must either be a player or
a mobile.  Awake characters in the room with your vision target will 'feel
a presence in the room'.

GROUP: {RDetection{x

The {RDraconian spell group{x deals with the magic of dragons -- in this case,
bringing forth the devastating power of their breath weapons upon your foes.
The {Rdraconian spell group{x contains the following:

{Cacid breath{x       uses the black dragon's attack upon an enemy
{Cfire breath{x       calls forth the flames of a red dragon
{Cfrost breath{x      drains the life from a foe with the power a white dragon
{Cgas breath{x        suffocates your enemies with a green dragon's fumes
{Clightning breath{x  summons the electrical fury of a blue dragon

keywords: ACID BREATH' 'FIRE BREATH' 'FROST BREATH' 'GAS BREATH' 'LIGHTNING BREATH
text:
Syntax:
   {Ccast{x '{Yacid breath{x'      <{Yvictim{x>
   {Ccast{x '{Yfire breath{x'      <{Yvictim{x>
   {Ccast{x '{Yfrost breath{x'     <{Yvictim{x>
   {Ccast{x '{Ylightning breath{x' <{Yvictim{x>
   {Ccast{x '{Ygas breath{x'       <{Yvictim{x>

These spells are for the use of dragons; however, high level mages may
also learn and cast these spells. Acid Breath and Lightning Breath
effect either the given {Yvictim{x or everyone who is currently fighting
the caster. Fire, Frost and Gas Breath effect all non-friendly targets
in the room.
{CFire{x and {Cfrost{x can break objects, and {Cacid{x can damage armor.

GROUP: {RDraconian{x

The {Renchantment spell group{x is used to imbue items with magical properties.
The {Renchantment spell group{x contains the following:

{Cenchant weapon{x     increases the hit and damage bonuses of a weapon
{Cenchant armor{x      increases the protective value of armor
{Cprotect container{x  permanantly protects a container, but for a price
{Crecharge{x           restores power to a depleted wand or staff
{Cremove invis{x       makes invisible items visible
{Cremove align{x       removes the alignemnt from objects
{Cshrink{x             reduces the weight of an object
{Cdazzling light{x     increases the hit and damage bonuses of a light

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yenchant weapon{x' <{Yweapon{x>

This spell magically enchants a weapon, increasing its to-hit and to-dam
bonuses by one or two points.  Multiple enchants may be cast, but as the
weapon grows more and more powerful, it is more likely to be drained or
destroyed by the magic.  Also, every successful enchant increases the level
of the weapon by one...and there is no turning back.  Casting this spell on
armor will work, but it can have unusual effects on the item.

GROUP: {REnchantment{x
{YSEE ALSO:{x enchant armor

Syntax:  {Ccast{x '{Yenchant armor{x' <{Yarmor{x>

The {Cenchant armor{x spell imbues armor with powerful protective magic.
It is not nearly as reliable as enchant weapon, being far more prone to
destructive effects.  Each successful enchant improves the armor bonus
of an item by 1 or 2 points, and raises its level by one.

GROUP: {REnchantment{x
{YSEE ALSO:{x enchant weapon

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yprotect container{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell makes a container immune to destruction.  However, the spell
costs a certain amount of gold depending on the capacity of the container.

GROUP: {REnchantment{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x {Yrecharge{Y <{Yitem{x>

The {Crecharge{x spell can be used to restore energy to depleted wands and
staves.  Fully exhausted items cannot be {Crecharged{x, and the difficulty
of the spell is proportional to the number of charges used.  Magical
items can only be {Crecharged{x once.

GROUP: {REnchantment{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yremove alignment{x' <{Yobject{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yremove invis{x' <{Yobject{x>

These spells change the properties of objects.	The {Cremove alignment{x
spell makes an object that was once anti-evil no longer anti-evil.
The same goes for anti-good and anti-neutral.  The {Cremove invisible{x
spell makes an object visible.

GROUP: {REnchantment{x
GROUP: {RBenedictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yshrink{x' <{Yobject{x>

This miracle spell can actually reduce the weight of an object.  The
object's level is increased by one for each successful casting.

GROUP: {REnchantment{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydazzling light{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell increases the hit and damage bonuses of a light source for a
limited time.  The amount depends on the caster's level.

GROUP: {REnchantment{x

The {Renhancement spells{x improve upon the body's pontential, allowing feats of
superhuman strength and speed.  However, these spells may often have harmful
effects upon the recipient.
The {Renhancement spell group{x contains the following:

{Cgiant strength{x       grants increased strength
{Chaste{x                doubles the speed of the target, but slows down healing
{Cinfravision{x          allows monsters to be seen in the dark
{Crefresh{x              restores energy to tired adventurers
{Cregeneration{x         doubles the healing rate of the target
{Ctalon{x                makes the target harder to disarm.
{Cdivine regeneration{x  quadruples the healing rate of the target
{Cchannel{x              channels mana from caster into the victim

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ygiant strength{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell increases the strength of the target character.

GROUP: {REnhancement{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yhaste{x' <{Ytarget{x>

The {Chaste{x spell increases the speed and agility of the recipient, allowing
an extra attack (or even a backstab) in combat, and improving evasive
abilities in combat.  However, it produces a great strain on the system,
such that recuperative abilities are halved.  {CHaste{x is capable of negating
the slow spell. (see 'help slow').

GROUP: {REnhancement{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yinfravision{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell enables the target character to see warm blooded creatures even
while in the dark, and exits of a room as well.

GROUP: {REnhancement{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yrefresh{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell heals the stamina of a character.

GROUP: {RHealing{x {REnhancements{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yregeneration{x' <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ydivine regeneration{x'

This spell increases the regeneration rate of the target character.
{CDivine regeneration{x can only be cast upon yourself and it also affects
mana.

{GEvolved:{x
When evolved, the {Cregeneration{x spell will cause stamina to heal 
during combat, and the {Cdivine regeneration{x spell causes stamina, 
mana, and hp to regen during combat.

GROUP: {REnhancement{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ytalon{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell makes the target character resistant to disarming.

{GEvolved:{x
When evolved, the {Ctalon{x spell strengthens the user's hands even more,
making it even harder to disarm that character.

GROUP: {REnhancement{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ychannel{x' <{Yamount{x> <{Ytarget{x>

This spell channels your magical energy into a target.

GROUP: {REnhancement{x

This spell group is the opposite of healing.  The {Rharmful spells{x are
designed to tear flesh from bone, rupture arteries, and generally turn the
body against itself.  
In order of power, the {Rharmful spells{x are:

{Ccause light{x     inflicts minor wounds on an enemy
{Ccause serious{x   inflicts wounds on a foe
{Ccause critical{x  causes major damage to the target
{Charm{x            the most deadly harmful spell

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycause light{x'    <{Yvictim{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ycause serious{x'  <{Yvictim{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ycause critical{x' <{Yvictim{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yharm{x'           <{Yvictim{x>

These spells inflict damage on the victim.  The higher-level spells do
more damage.

GROUP: {RHarmful{x

The {Rhealing spells{x are used to cure the wounds that adventurers inevitably
suffer in battle.  For curing other conditions, such as poison, see the
{Wcurative{x spell group.  
In order of power, the {Rhealing spells{x are:

{Crefresh{x         restores vigor to a tired adventurer
{Ccure light{x      heals minor wounds
{Ccure serious{x    heals wounds
{Ccure critical{x   closes all but the worst wounds
{Cheal{x            the most powerful healing spell
{Cmass healing{x    casts a heal spell on each player in the room
{Cdivine healing{x  calls forth the power of the gods to perform a miracle

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycure light{x'    <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ycure serious{x'  <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ycure critical{x' <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yheal{x'          <{Ycharacter{x>

These spells cure damage on the target character.  The higher-level spells
heal more damage.

GROUP: {RHealing{x

{YSEE ALSO:{x healer

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ymass healing{x'

The {Cmass healing{x spell, as its name might suggest, performs a healing spell
on all players in the room.  It also throws in a refresh spell for good
measure.

GROUP: {RHealing{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydivine healing{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell brings forth the power of the gods to heal a victim in need.
As the name implies, it is much more powerful than 'heal'.

GROUP: {RHealing{x

The {Rillusions spells{x are dedicated to deception and trickery.  They can be
used to mask appearances, or create distractions for the party.  Currently,
the {Rillusion group{x is very small, but it will be expanded in the future.
The {Rillusion spell group{x consist of the following:

{Cinvis{x          turns the target invisible
{Cmass invis{x     turns the caster's group invisible
{Cventriloquate{x  allows the caster to put words in someone's mouth
{Csmokescreen{x    creates a mysterious cloud of smoke in adjacent rooms

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yinvisibility{x' <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Yinvisibility{x' <{Yobject{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ymass invis{x'

The {Cinvisibility{x spell makes the target character invisible.  Invisible
characters will become visible when they attack.  You may also cast the
spell on an object to render it invisible.

The {Cmass invis{x spell makes all characters in the caster's group invisible,
including the caster.

GROUP: {RIllusion{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yventriloquate{x' <{Yspeaker{x> <{Ymessage{x>

This spell throws your voice, making it appear that some other object
or character in the room is saying your message.  Victims who make
their saving throw will know that someone is using ventriloquism, but
not who.  Victims who fail their saving throw will think that the object
or character really did say your message.

GROUP: {RIllusion{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ysmokescreen{x' <{Ydirection{x>

This spell conjures a cloud of smoke in an adjacent room, and all mobiles
in that room will not be able to see you for a short period of time.

GROUP: {RIllusion{x

{RMaladictions{x are a group of curses and other baneful spells, designed to
cripple, inconvenience, or torture, rather than kill outright.  
The {Rmaladictions spell group{x consist of the following:

{Cblindness{x     strikes the target blind
{Ccurse{x         prevents recalling and weakens the target in combat
{Cenergy drain{x  drains experiance and mana, while strengthening the caster
{Cplague{x        causes the target to suffer a slow, painful death from plague
{Cpoison{x        weaker than plague, but often fatal
{Cslow{x          slows your enemies down, reducing their rate of attack	
{Cweaken{x        drains the strength of the target
{Cage{x           ages your victim

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yblindness{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell clouds the eyes of the target, making them unable to see.

GROUP: {RMaladictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ycurse{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell reduces the character's to-hit roll and save versus spells.
It also renders the character unclean in the eyes of Mota and unable
to RECALL. Curse may be used to fill equipment with evil power, allowing
(for example) weapons to do more damage to particularly holy opponents.

GROUP: {RMaladictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yenergy drain{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell saps the experience points, mana, and movement points of its
target, and transfers them to you (except the experience).

GROUP:{RMaladictions{x {RNecromancy{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x {Yslow{x <{Ytarget{x>

Following popular mythology, {Cslow{x is the opposite of haste.  It is a spell
with it's own unique set of effects.  When cast on an unfortunate victim,
it slows its movements, making it easier to hit and reducing its rate
of attack.  The effect of {Cslow{x also double movement costs and double
healing rates.

GROUP: {RMaladictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x {Yplague{x <{Ytarget{x>

The {Cplague{x spell infests the target with a magical disease of great virulence,
sapping its strength and causing horrific suffering, possibly leading to
death.  It is a risky spell to use, as the contagion can spread like
wildfire if the victim makes it to a populated area.

GROUP: {RMaladictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ypoison{x' <{Yvictim{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ypoison{x' <{Yobject{x>

This spell reduces the strength of the victim by two, as well as reducing
the victim's regeneration rate. It may also be used to poison food, drink,
or a weapon in a fashion similar to envenom ('{Rhelp envenom{x'), but with
drastically reduced effectiveness.

GROUP: {RMaladictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yweaken{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell reduces the strength of the victim.

GROUP: {PMaladictions{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yage{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell reduces the strength and constitution of the victim while
increasing their wisdom.  It also temporarily ages the victim by a number
of years equal to the level of the caster.

GROUP: {RMaladictions{x

The {Rnecromancy spell group{x is rooted in the black magic of evil mages.
Mages who drew their power from the evil sides of their tainted souls.
These mages were able to use this power to summon undead creatures to
do their bidding.  Once summoned, these undead monsters would fight
to protect their master.
The {Rnecromancy spell group{x consist of the following:

{Cblood moon{x        this spell summons the power of the vampires
{Canimate skeleton{x  summons an undead skeleton to your side
{Canimate zombie{x    summons a powerful zombie warrior to your side
{Canimate wraith{x    summons a ghastly wraith to your side
{Canimate gargoyle{x  summons a deadly gargoyle mage to your side
{Cfear{x              this spell makes a victim call for their mother
{Cresurrect{x         a high level spell that brings the dead to life
{Cenergy drain{x      drains exp and mana, while strengthening the caster

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yblood moon{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell invokes the powers of the vampire into a mortal, and should
only be used upon those who serve evil well.

GROUP: {RNecromancy{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yanimate skeleton{x'
        {Ccast{x '{Yanimate zombie{x'
        {Ccast{x '{Yanimate wraith{x'
        {Ccast{x '{Yanimate gargoyle{x'

These spells summon forth the power and abilities of the undead.  Only
one mobile of each type may be summoned.  However, all of these animated
creatures can be summoned for a single battle.	Once their time in this
world has come to an end, these undead creatures will return to the
netherworld from whence they came.

GROUP: {RNecromancy{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yfear{x' <{Yvictim{x>

This spell adversely affects the victim's to-hit roll, damage roll, and
saves vs spells.  Terror grips the victim's mind and they will flee if at
all possible.

GROUP: {RNecromancy{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yresurrect{x' <{Ycorpse{x>

This spell, when cast upon a corpse in the room, will create a zombie
to rise and become your servant.

GROUP: {RNecromancy{x

The {Rprotective spells{x are used to shield against harm, whether from spells or
physical attack.  They range from the weak armor spell to the sought-after
sanctuary charm.  Also included in this group are several spells for dispeling
hostile magics.  The {Rprotective spell group{x consists of the following spells:

{Carmor{x            provides the target with an extra layer of defense
{Ccancellation{x     a powerful dispel, used for removing spells from friends
{Cdispel magic{x     removes spells from enemies, not as effective as cancel
{Cundo spell{x       similar to cancellation, except it is spell specific
{Cfireproof{x        shields items from the harmful effects of fire and acid
{Cprotection evil{x  provides defense from the attacks of evil creatures
{Cprotection good{x  protects from the attacks of good beings
{Csanctuary{x        reduces all damage taken by the recipient by half
{Cshield{x           puts a shimmering shield between you and your enemies
{Cstoneskin{x        turns skin hard as stone, providing a huge armor boost

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yarmor{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell decreases (improves) the armor class of the target character
by 20 points.

GROUP: {RProtective{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ydispel magic{x' <{Ycharacter{x>
        {Ccast{x '{Ycancellation{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

Both of these spells attempt to remove magical effects from the target.
{CDispel magic{x has a lower chance of working, and is considered an attack
spell.  {CCancellation{x can only be used on allies, but is more effective
and does not provoke attack.  Unfortunately, neither of these spells will
discriminate between harmful and benign spells.

The chance of dispelling is based on the level of the spell and the saves
of the victim.  Permanent spells (such as mobile sanctuary) are much more
difficult to remove. Certain spells may not be dispelled at all, such as
plague and poison.

GROUP: {RProtective{x
{YSEE ALSO:{x 'undo spell'

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yundo spell{x' <{Yspell{x> <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell is similar to cancellation except that it is spell specific.

GROUP: {RProtective{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x {Yfireproof{x <{Yobject{x>

The {Cfireproof{x spell creates a short-lived protective aura around an
object, to protect it from the harmful effects spells.  Items protected
by this spell are not harmed by fire or the heat metal spell.  Although
inexpensive to use, the spell's short duration makes it impractical for
protecting large numbers of objects.

GROUP: {RProtective{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yprotection evil{x'
	{C       cast{x '{Yprotection good{x'

The {Cprotection{x spells reduce the amount of damage taken from attackers
of the appropriate ethos, and improve saving throws against all forms of
magic. They may not be cast on others.

{GEvolved:
{xWhen evolved, the {Cprotection{x spells can {Ydaze{x your enemies, lowering
their chances of successful spellcasting and skill usage.  At higher
evolution, {Cprotection{x can even {Ystun{x your opponent, preventing them
from taking action at all.

GROUP: {RProtective{x

attack by roughly one half.

{GEvolved:{x
When evolved, the {Csanctuary{x spell can reduce even more damage, weakens
attacks by cursed characters and sometimes even stops them cold, and can
deal holy damage to the attacker for violating the protective aura.

GROUP: {RProtective{x

Scry allows you to see the contents of a room where your
pet is currently located. This can be used by leaving your pet

armor class.  {CShield{x provides 20 points of armor.  {CStone skin{x provides
40 points of armor.  You may cast {Cshield{x but not {Cstone skin{x on another.

GROUP: {RProtective{x

The {Rtransportation group{x is used for travel, whether by flight, magical
teleportation, or walking through walls.  These spells are among the most 
useful in the game.  The {Rtransportation spell group{x consists of the 
following spells:

{Cfly{x              allows the target to fly over nearly all obstacles
{Cgate{x             transports the caster to the target
{Cnexus{x            forms a two-way portal to a far off destination
{Cpass door{x        allows the caster to walk through walls
{Cportal{x           creates a one-way portal to a destination
{Csummon{x           transports the target to the caster
{Csummon object{x    transports an object to the caster
{Cteleport{x         sends the target to a random location
{Cteleport object{x  sends obejcts from one character to another
{Cword of recall{x   transports the caster to safety in Midgaard

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yfly{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

This spell enables the target character to fly.

GROUP: {RTransportation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ynexus{x' <{Ytarget{x>

This spell is virtually identical to {Wportal{x, with the only difference
being that while portal creates a one-way gate, a {Cnexus{x spell makes a
two-sided gate.  It also lasts longer than the lower-powered portal
spell.  Both spells require an additional power source, the secret
of which has been lost...

GROUP: {RTransportation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ypass door{x' <{Ytarget{x>

This spell enables the caster to become translucent in order to pass through
closed doors.  Casting {cpass door{x without a target will target yourself.

GROUP: {RTransportation{x

Syntax: cast '{Ygate{x' <{Ycharacter/mob{x>

This spell is one of the most basic transportation spells.  It allows you
to teleport yourself and your pet (if you have one) to another character
or mob by use of a magical gate, with certain limitations.  You cannot
{Cgate{x to a monster or player that is more than 3 levels higher than you,
and monsters recieve a save against {Cgate{x.  You also cannot {Cgate{x to rooms
belonging to Immortals, private rooms, and rooms that are no-recall, nor
can you {cgate{x out of norecall rooms.

GROUP: {RTransportation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Yportal{x' <{Ytarget{x>

The {Cportal{x spell is similar to gate, but creates a lasting one-way
portal to the target creature instead of transporting the caster.  Portals
are entered using '{WENTER{x', as in 'enter portal'.  Portals cannot be made
to  certain destinations, but can be used to reach norecall rooms. Portal
requires a special source of power to be used, unfortunately the secret of
this material component has been lost...

GROUP: {RTransportation{x

Syntax: {Ccast{x '{Ysummon{x' <{Ycharacter{x>

room.  Characters who are fighting may not be summoned.

{YSEE ALSO:{x nosummon

world to the earth below your feet.  Objects cannot be summoned from
places you cannot see, from powerful mobiles, from safe or private
rooms, or from other players.  The object must also be close to your
power level.

        {Ccast{x '{Yteleport object{x' <{Yobject{x> <{Yperson{x>

{CTeleport object{x will send an object across great distances to another.

These spells allow the caster to manipulate local weather conditions, as well

{Ccall lightning{x   summons a huge bolt from the heavens, if the weather is right

(the weather command, unrelated to this group, shows local weather conditions)

GROUP: {RWeather{x

Syntax : {Ccast{x '{Yblizzard{x' <{Ytarget{x>

The {CBlizzard{x spell is well suited to demonstrating the power of nature.
A flurry of ice and snow originates from the hands of the caster and
quickly snowballs into an avalanche which rebounds between targets in

 3370 The School of Skills       <> 8543 The Gathering             (Playpen )

Syntax:  {Csedit delete         {W<{Vsocial{W>
         {Csedit new            {W<{Vsocial{W>
         {Csedit show           {W<{Vsocial{W>
         {Csedit {W<{Ystring-name{W>  <{Vsocial{W> {W[{Gstring{W]{x

{CSEDIT{x allows online creation, deletion and editting of socials.

{CSEDIT DELETE{x <social> deletes that social. The deletion is irrecoverable,
save for restoring a backup file, so use this command with great care!

{CSEDIT NEW <social>{x creates a new, empty social.

{CSEDIT SHOW <social>{x shows all of the strings for that social.

{CSEDIT <string-name> <social> [string]{x changes a certain of the 7 strings that
make up a social. If you leave out the string, no message will be sent to
the involved characters.

There are 7 strings that make up a social:
{Ycnoarg: Sent to character when no argument typed.
onoarg: Sent to others in the room when no argument is given.
cfound: Sent to the character if a target is found.
vfound: Sent to the victim.
ofound: Sent to others in room, if target is found.
cself : Sent to the character if the target is self.
oself : Sent to others in room when a player targets themselves.{x

To get the player names and sexes correct, you need to use those macros:

{c/{GItem      {c/{BFor Char	{c/{PFor Victim
{c|----------------------------------\
{c|{Gname       {B$n           {P$N        {c|
{c|{Ghe/she     {B$e           {P$E        {c|
{c|{Ghim/her    {B$m           {P$M        {c|
{c|{Ghis/hers   {B$s           {P$S{x        {c|
{c|{G(his/her)                         {c|
{c\----------------------------------/

So, to make a new social called 'epoke' which would poke eyes out, you would
do the following:

{WSEDIT NEW epoke
SEDIT cnoarg epoke Poke whose eyes out?
SEDIT onoarg epoke $n looks for a pair of eyes to poke out.
SEDIT cfound epoke You poke $N's eyes out!
SEDIT vfound epoke $n pokes your eyes out! Ouch..
SEDIT ofound epoke $n pokes $N's eyes out!
SEDIT cself  epoke Being bored, you poke your own eyes out.
SEDIT oself  epoke $n pokes $s eyes out!{x

To get more examples, type {GSEDIT SHOW <social-name>{x to see one of the
existing socials.

The socials are stored in a file called socials.txt. Manual editting of
that file is not advised. A frequent back-up is recommended. The social
file is updated by the server as soon as anything changes.  Because of
this code, there is no longer any option for if the player you try to
do the action on is not there.


{cOnline social editting - (c) 1996,97 Erwin S. Andreasen <erwin@pip.dknet.dk>{x

Syntax:  {Pcedit delete         {W<{Vclan{W>
         {Pcedit new            {W<{Vclan{W>
	 {Pcedit show           {W<{Vclan{W>
	 {Pcedit {W<{Ystring-name{W>  <{Vclan{W> {W[{Gstring{W]{x

The 'new' commands adds a clan to the collection of clans. In order to create
a new clan, you have to provide the clan's name. Choose this name wisely for
it will be the clan's mail name and this name will be used for any clan-
related command. This name can {PNOT{x be altered once created.
All newly created clan start with 100 questpoints.
The 'delete' command on clans can only be used by the IMPLEMENTOR.
The 'show' command displays the specified clan on the screen.

You can edit a clan by specifying the name of the field you want to change
and the new value.

There are 8 values that make up a clan:
{Ywhoname    {x: the name of the clan as it should show in 'who'.
{Yhall       {x: the vnum of the room the 'clanrecall' commands leads to.
{Yareamin    {x: the lower vnum of the area the clanhall is in.
{Yareamax    {x: the upper vnum of the area the clanhall is in.
{Yindependent{x: indicates if it's a loner clan. Always use '0'.
{Yclanqp     {x: the number of questpoints a clan has gathered.
{Yclanname   {x: the fullname of the clan.
{Ycreator    {x: the name(s) of the creator(s) of the clan.{x

The whoname, clanname and creator are strings, the other fields are numerical.
for independent, use 1 for Yes, and 0 for No.
The number specified for clanrecall has to be an existing room in the mud.

{PWARNING: DO NOT CREATE CLANS JUST FOR FUN! THIS WILL BE PUNISHED WITH A
         DEMOTION!{x

The mud is constantly allocating memory as it needs it.  However, the mud
rarely deallocates that memory.  Instead, it frees up the memory and holds
it in storage for later use.  Then in the future, when the mud needs more
memory, it grabs this reserved memory and uses it.

Now, if there is no reserved memory of the type the mud needs, it will
allocate more memory.  This is what causes the Wiznet {PMalloc{x message to
appear.  Normally this is not a problem.  Especially when the mud has just
started or is returning from a copyover.  You will notice that as the first
characters log in, you get spammed by tons of messages.  The problem is when
24 hours later you are still getting memory allocation messages.

When this happens, it is an indication of a memory leak. If you see {PMalloc{x
messages pop up after the mud has been running for several days, please
note to Imms about it.  However, keep in mind, that it still may not be a
problem.  i.e. The mud just increased to an all time high of 30 players for
that day, or a player is posting a new note (since notes normally don't
disappear, memory is almost always allocated for new notes).

Syntax: {Cmemory{x

{CMemory{x reports the size of your mud.

The 'Mobs' and 'Objs' lines report the number of types of mobiles and
objects, not the number of instances currently in the game.

The 'Strings' line shows memory used by area-file strings.  These strings
have a maximum limit, which is also shown.

The 'Perms' line shows permanently allocated memory blocks.  There is no
limit on the number and size of these blocks.

Syntax:  {Cshutdown{x <{Yreason{x>

{CShutdown{x shuts down the server and prevents the normal 'startup' script
from restarting it.  The reason can be any brief message that explains why
there is going to be a {Vshutdown{x.

{YSEE ALSO:{x reboot, autoreboot

Syntax: {Cautoreboot{x <{Ytime{x>

For an {Cautoreboot{x, select a time between 0000 and 2399.  If you select a
time that is earlier than the current time, the mud will assume the next
day for the date.  When the reboot time matches the current time, the
mud will check to see if any players are logged in.  If there are no players,
then the {Creboot{x will occur.  If there are players, the the mud will advance
the reboot time ahead by 5 minutes and wait to check again.  {CAutoreboot{x
without an argument will display the current autoreboot status.

NOTE: Immortal characters are ignored when counting for players in the
{Cautoreboot{x command.

{YSEE ALSO:{x shutdown, reboot

Syntax: {Cdisable{x
        {Cdisable{x <{Ycommand{x> <{Yreason{x>

{CDisable{x without an argument will show a list of disabled commands.
{CDisable{x with an argument will either disable or enable a command.  The
command must be spelled out in its entirety, and you must provide a short
explanation as to why you disabled the command.

When a command is disabled, no players can use it. It is useful for
disabling buggy commands or disabling certain commands for use with quests.

To re-able a command, use "disable <{Ycommand{x>".

Syntax: {Creboot{x <{Yreason{x>

{CReboot{x shuts down the server.  When the normal 'startup' script is used
to control the server, a delay of fifteen seconds will ensue (to allow
old connections to die), followed by a {Creboot{x.

{YSEE ALSO:{x shutdown, autoreboot

Syntax: {Cadjust{x <{Yvictim{x> <{Yexp points{x>

This command will raise or lower a player's exp by a given amount.  This
command is only used for testing purposes on test characters.  This
command is {Pnever{x to be used on a mortal player.

Syntax: {Cadvance{x <{Ycharacter{x> <{Ylevel{x>

{CAdvance{x sets a character's level to the specified value.  It is the
only way to create new immortal characters within the game.  {CAdvance{x
may also be used to demote characters.

Syntax: {Cdoas{x <{Ycharacter{x> <{Ycommands{x>

{CDoas{x is an unobtrusive way for an immortal to check information available
to a player.  It gives no message to the player, and can be used for such
things as seeing their practice list, gain list, etc.  {CDoas{x is {PNOT{x a toy,
and should never be used to force players to do things as a joke.

Syntax: {Ctrust{x <{Ycharacter{x> <{Ylevel{x>

{CTrust{x sets a character's trust to the specified level.  This is similar to
advancing the character for accessing immortal commands, but has no effect
on combat strength, etc.  You can give more trust to mortal (or immortal)
characters to give them access to more power without messing up the
character.  You can also restrict a player's trust to less than their
natural level.

A trust of 0 means to use the character's natural level again.

Syntax: {Cdebug lcheck{x
        {Cdebug qtz{x
        {Cdebug actflag{x
        ... etc.

The {Cdebug{x command is used to connect to a handful of special temporary
commands used mainly by the coders to find a certain bug in the game, or
to fix one. Subcommands, like {Rlcheck{x, {Rqtz{x and {Ractflag{x may
come and go as needed, may be very sparsely documented and may not do
what you wanted. This command is intended for maintenance and debugging
purposes only, not day-to-day game control.
The current subfunctions do the following:
lcheck - checks on the location of all objects in lockers, was once
         instrumental in fixing the locker memory bug.
qtz    - sets a player's time to next quest to 0. This was used to
         test the new quest code. Any use of this subcommand outside of
         a testing situation constitutes cheating.
actflag - Given the NUMERIC value of an ACT flag, tells you how many
          mobs in the game have that flag. Used to check for available
          flags for new properties. Yes, our forefathers have used some
          flags they forgot to document, and it took this command to
          find them.

Syntax: {Cdump{x

Creates a {Cdump{x file for mobiles, objects, and memory.

Syntax: {Cpipe{x <{Ycommand{x>

{YThe {Cpipe{Y command is reserved for implementors.{x  It is a way to send a single
command to the unix shell, and therefore should *never* be given to those
without shell access.

{YExamples of use:{x

    {Mpipe {Yfinger        {CShows what accounts are logged in to Kyndig.
    {Mpipe {Yquota         {CShows total and maximum disk space, in the 'blocks' and 'quota'
                       columns.
    {Mpipe {Yps -ux        {CShows a list of current processes, such as ftp connections,
                       shell connections, startup scripts, and spawn.
    {Mpipe {Ykill {x<{VPID{x>    {CUsed to terminate a process, usually used with runaway startup
                       scripts and spawn.{x

{PIf you do not know what you are doing, do NOT use {Cpipe{P for any more than the above
reasons.{x

{CBattle start{x and {Cstop{x will determine when mortals may attack each other
